introSection: Raptor is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio, USA. The ride was built by Bolliger & Mabillard and opened in May 1994. It was the world's tallest and fastest inverted roller coaster at the time. The attraction was also the first inverted roller coaster to feature a cobra roll.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
On September 1, 1993, it was announced that Raptor would be coming to Cedar Point for the 1994 season.

Raptor opened to the general public on May 7, 1994.

On August 13, 2015, a 45-year-old guest was struck and killed by a Raptor train after entering a restricted area to retrieve his belongings. The ride reopened the following day.

fullText:
Raptor is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio, USA. The ride was built by Bolliger & Mabillard and opened in May 1994. It was the world's tallest and fastest inverted roller coaster at the time. The attraction was also the first inverted roller coaster to feature a cobra roll.

On September 1, 1993, it was announced that Raptor would be coming to Cedar Point for the 1994 season.

Raptor opened to the general public on May 7, 1994.

On August 13, 2015, a 45-year-old guest was struck and killed by a Raptor train after entering a restricted area to retrieve his belongings. The ride reopened the following day.



Lime track and green supports.
